headline: Morgan Stanley warns chips look like silver
detail: Morgan Stanley's Wilson is drawing parallels between chip stocks and silver — a commodity prone to sharp, sentiment-driven collapses. This is a direct warning shot at your heaviest sector exposure: AVGO, SMH, and CRWD-adjacent tech names. If institutional money starts rotating out of semis on this narrative, your already-losing AVGO position gets worse fast.

headline: 67% bear market signal flashing now
detail: A rare technical or macro signal is giving 2-in-3 odds of a bear market, according to this report. For a margin trader concentrated in high-beta tech and chips, a bear market isn't a 20% drawdown — it's a margin call scenario. This raises urgency on your SELL-flagged names and your leveraged positions like SPAL.

headline: AI boom faces severe weather threat
detail: Severe weather is being flagged as a new operational risk for AI infrastructure — data centers, power grids, supply chains. AVGO and the broader chip/AI complex depend on this infrastructure, so it's a slow-burn risk for your core names rather than a one-day event.

headline: Trump-Iran talks resume in Qatar
detail: Fresh US-Iran talks could ease energy and geopolitical tensions, which generally supports risk-on sentiment. However, any breakdown in talks would spike uncertainty and hit growth/tech names disproportionately — worth monitoring as a tail risk for your book this week.
